english: the shape of a basket repeatedly woven from bamboo

etymology.description: two fish йӯҡ(йӯҡ) (yГә, "fish") with mouths facing each other -> original script of йҒҳ(йҒҳ) (gГІu, "to meet; to encounter")
etymology.interpretation: The character еҶ“(gГІu) initially depicted two fish facing each other. This visual representation symbolized the idea of meeting or encountering, which is the original meaning of the character. Over time, this concept was associated with encountering or interactions, as two fish facing each other suggest a meeting.
